source_refs.html: /Users/toy/English/躺着学/躺着学阅读/8053_Passage 2 Leaf-Cutting Ants And Fungus.html

passages.title: Leaf-Cutting Ants And Fungus
passages.source_html:
Leaf-Cutting Ants and Fungus


A


The ants and their agriculture have been extensively studied over the years, but the recent research has uncovered intriguing new findings of the fungus they cultivate, how they domesticated it and how they cultivate it and preserve it from pathogens. For example, the fungus farms, which the ants were thought to keep free of pathogens, turn out to be vulnerable to a devastating mold, found nowhere else but in ants' nests. To keep the mold in check, the ants long ago made a discovery that would do credit to any pharmaceutical laboratory.


B


Leaf-cutting ants and their fungus farms are a marvel of nature and perhaps the best-known example of symbiosis, the mutual dependence of two species. The ant's achievement is remarkable – the biologist Edward O. Wilson has called it 'one of the major breakthroughs in animal evolution' – because it allows them to eat, courtesy of their mushroom's digestive powers, the otherwise poisoned harvest of tropical forests whose leaves are laden with terpenoids, alkaloids and other chemicals designed to sicken browsers. 


C


Fungus growing seems to have originated only once in evolution because all gardening ants belong to a single tribe, the descendants of the first fungus farmer. There are more than 200 known species of the attine ant tribe, divided into 12 groups, or genera. The leaf-cutters use fresh vegetation; the other groups, known as the lower attines because their nests are smaller and their techniques more primitive, feed their gardens with detritus like dead leaves, insects and faeces. In 1994 a team of four biologists, Ulrich G. Mueller and Ted R. Schultz from Cornell University and Ignacio H. Chapela and Stephen A. Rehner from the United States Department of Agriculture, ananlyzed the DNA of ant funguses. The common assumption that the funguses are all derived from a single strain, they found, was only half true.


D


The leaf-cutters' fungus was indeed descended from a single strain, propagated clonally, or just by budding, for at least 23 million years. But the lower attine ants used different varieties of the fungus, and in one case a quite separate species, the four biologists discovered. The pure strain of fungus grown by the leaf-cutters, it seemed to Mr Currie, resembled the monocultures of various human crops, that are very productive for a while and then succumb to some disastrous pathogen, such as the Irish potato blight. Monocultures, which lack the genetic diversity to respond to changing environmental threats, are sitting ducks for parasites. Mr Currie felt there had to be a parasite in the ant-fungus system. But a century of ant research offered no support for the idea. Textbooks describe how leaf-cutter ants scrupulously weed their gardens of all foreign organisms. 'People kept telling me, ' You know the ants keep their gardens free of parasites, don't you?’’ Mr Currie said of his efforts to find a hidden interloper.


E


But after three years of sifting through attine ant gardens, Mr Currie discovered they are far from free of infection. In last month's issue of the Proceeding of the National Academy of Sciences, he and two colleagues, Dr Mueller and David Mairoch, isolated several alien organisms, particularly a family of parasitic molds called Escovopsis. Escovopsis turns out to be a highly virulent pathogen that can devastate a fungus garden in a couple of days. It blooms like a while cloud, with the garden dimly visible underneath. In a day or two, the whole garden is enveloped. 'Other ants won't go near it and the ants associated with the garden just starve to death,' Dr. Rehner said. 'They just seem to give up, except for those that have rescued their larvae.’ The deadly mold then turns greenish-brown as it enters its spore-forming stage.


F


Evidently, the ants usually manage to keep Escovopsis and other parasites under control. But with any lapse in control, or if the ants are removed, Escovopsis will quickly burst forth. Although new leaf-cutter gardens start off free of Escovopsis, within two years some 60 percent become infected. The discovery of Escovopsis's role brings a new level of understanding of the evolution of the attine ants. 'In the last decade, evolutionary biologists have been increasingly aware of the role of parasites as driving forces in evolution,' Dr Schultz said. There is now a possible reason to explain why the lower attine species keep changing the variety of fungus in their mushroom gardens, and occasionally domesticating new ones – to stay one step ahead of the relentless Escovopsis.


G


Interestingly, Mr. Currie found that the leaf-cutters had in general fewer alien molds in their gardens than the lower attines, yet they had more Escovopsis infections. It seems that the price they pay for cultivating a pure variety of fungus is a higher risk from Escovopsis. But the leaf-cutters may have a little alternative: they cultivate a special variety of fungus which, unlike those grown by the lower attines, produces nutritious swollen tips for the ants to eat.


H


Discovery of a third partner in the ant-fungus symbiosis raises the question of how the attine ants, especially the leaf-cutters, keep this dangerous interloper under control. Amazingly enough, Mr Currie has again provided the answer. 'People have known for a hundred years that ants have a whitish growth on the cuticle,' said Dr Mueller, referring to the insects' body surface. 'People would say this is like a cuticular wax. But Cameron was the first one in a hundred years to put these things under a microscope. He saw it was not inert wax. It is alive.' Mr Currie discovered a specialized patch on the ants' cuticle that harbors a particular kind of bacterium, one well known to the pharmaceutical industry, because it is the source of half the antibiotics used in medicine. From each of 22 species of attine ant studied, Mr. Cameron and colleagues isolated a species of Streptomyces bacterium, they reported in Nature in April. The Streptomyces does not have much effect on ordinary laboratory funguses. But it is a potent poisoner of Escovopsis, inhibiting its growth and suppressing spore formation. It also stimulates the growth of the ants' mushroom fungus. The bacterium is carried by virgin queens when they leave to establish new nests but is not found on male ants, playboys who take no responsibility in nest-making or gardening. Because both the leaf-cutters and the lower attines use Streptomyces, the bacterium may have been part of their symbiosis for almost as long as the Escovopsis mold. If so, some Alexander Fleming of an ant discovered antibiotics millions of years before people did. Even now, the ants are accomplishing two feats beyond the powers of human technology. The leaf-cutters are growing a monocultural crop year after year without disaster, and they are using an antibiotic apparently so wisely and prudently that, unlike people, they are not provoking antibiotic resistance in the target pathogen.

groups.title: Questions 14-26
groups.source_html:
Questions 14-19


Use the information in the passage to match the options (listed A-C) with activities or features of ants below. 

Choose the appropriate letters A-C in boxes 14-19. 

NB You may use any letter more than once.


A Leaf-cutting ants

B Lower attines

C Both


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
14

14 build small nests and live with the different foreign fungus.
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：The other groups, known as the lower attines because their nests are smaller and their techniques more primitive, feed their gardens with detritus like dead leaves, insects and faeces. But the lower attine ants used different varieties of the fungus, and in one case a quite separate species.


审题：题目询问哪种蚂蚁建造小巢穴并与不同的外来真菌一起生活。


定位关键信息：利用题目中的"build small nests"定位到C段中的"nests are smaller"，描述lower attines的巢穴较小；利用"live with the different foreign fungus"定位到D段中的"used different varieties of the fungus"，说明lower attines使用不同种类的真菌。


解题思路：在C段，文章明确指出lower attines的巢穴较小；在D段，进一步说明lower attines使用不同品种的真菌，包括一个完全不同的物种。结合这两点，可以确定lower attines符合题目的描述，因此答案为B，代表Lower attines。
15

15 use toxic leaves to feed the fungus
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：because it allows them to eat, courtesy of their mushroom’s digestive powers, the otherwise poisoned harvest of tropical forests whose leaves are laden with terpenoids, alkaloids and other chemicals designed to sicken browsers.


审题：题目询问哪种蚂蚁使用有毒的叶子来喂养它们培养的真菌，选项包括A Leaf-cutting ants、B Lower attines或C Both。


定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词“toxic leaves”定位到文章中的相关描述，在B段找到“poisoned harvest”和“leaves are laden with terpenoids, alkaloids”等短语，这些表示叶子有毒，同时结合“feed the fungus” implied在蚂蚁通过真菌消化有毒叶子的过程中。


解题思路：在B段中，句子明确指出leaf-cutting ants能够食用热带森林的有毒叶子，这得益于它们真菌的消化能力；这些叶子富含terpenoids、alkaloids等化学物质， designed to sicken browsers，表明叶子有毒。蚂蚁使用这些叶子喂养真菌，然后食用真菌。相比之下，C段提到lower attines使用detritus如死叶、昆虫和粪便喂养真菌，但未指定这些叶子有毒，因此只有leaf-cutting ants符合描述，答案选A。
16

16 raise a single fungus which doesn't live with other foreigners.
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：Textbooks describe how leaf-cutter ants scrupulously weed their gardens of all foreign organisms.  


审题：题目询问哪种蚂蚁培养的真菌不与其他外来物共存，选项为A Leaf-cutting ants、B Lower attines或C Both。  


定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"raise fungus"和"doesn’t live with other foreigners"定位到文章D段，该段描述了leaf-cutter ants如何清除花园中的外来生物。  


解题思路：在D段中，明确提到leaf-cutter ants会仔细清除其真菌花园中的所有外来生物，这表明它们培养的真菌环境是纯净的，不与其他外来物共存。而lower attines使用碎屑如死叶和昆虫喂养花园，可能引入外来物，因此不符合描述。故答案确定为A Leaf-cutting ants。
17

17 use substance to fight against Escovopsis.
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：Because both the leaf-cutters and the lower attines use Streptomyces


审题：题目询问哪种蚂蚁使用一种物质来对抗Escovopsis霉菌。  


定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词“use substance to fight against Escovopsis”定位到文章中关于Streptomyces细菌的描述，特别是在H段中明确提到both leaf-cutters and lower attines use Streptomyces，这种细菌用于对抗Escovopsis。

  

解题思路：在H段中，句子“Because both the leaf-cutters and the lower attines use Streptomyces”直接表明两种蚂蚁都使用Streptomyces，这是一种抗生素细菌，能抑制Escovopsis的生长和孢子形成，从而对抗这种病原体。因此，两种蚂蚁都使用这种物质，所以正确答案是C Both。
18

18 use dead vegetation to feed the fungus
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：the other groups, known as the lower attines because their nests are smaller and their techniques more primitive, feed their gardens with detritus like dead leaves, insects and faeces.  


审题：题目询问哪种蚂蚁使用死去的植物来喂养真菌。  


定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词“dead vegetable”定位到材料C段中的“dead leaves”，该部分描述蚂蚁的喂养方式。

  

解题思路：在C段中，明确区分了leaf-cutting ants使用新鲜植被，而lower attines使用碎屑如死叶、昆虫和粪便来喂养真菌。死叶属于死去的植物材料，因此只有lower attines符合这一描述，答案选择B。
19

19 are free of parasites explained previously.
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：Textbooks describe how leaf-cutter ants scrupulously weed their gardens of all foreign organisms. 和 People kept telling me, ‘You know the ants keep their gardens free of parasites, don’t you?’’


审题：题目询问哪个选项（A Leaf-cutting ants, B Lower attines, C Both）在以前被解释为没有寄生虫。


定位关键信息：利用题目的关键词“free of parasites”在文章中定位，搜索到D段中出现“keep their gardens free of parasites”的描述，直接关联到leaf-cutter ants。


解题思路：在D段中，教科书和人们以前的观点认为leaf-cutter ants会仔细清除花园中的所有外来生物，保持花园没有寄生虫，这对应选项A Leaf-cutting ants。虽然后来研究证明实际情况并非如此，但题目问的是“explained previously”即以前的解释，因此确定答案为A。

Questions 20-24

The reading passage has eight paragraphs A-H.

Which paragraph contains the following information? Choose the correct letter, A-H, in boxes 20-24.

Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
20

20 Dangerous outcome of Escovopsis
✅ 正确答案：E

原文答案句：Mr Currie discovered they are far from free of infection. In last month’s issue of the Proceeding of the National Academy of Sciences, he and two colleagues, Dr Mueller and David Mairoch, isolated several alien organisms, particularly a family of parasitic molds called Escovopsis. 

 

审题：题目要求找出哪个段落包含了Escovopsis的危险后果的信息。 


定位关键信息：使用题目中的关键词“Escovopsis”和“dangerous outcome”进行定位。在文章中扫描“Escovopsis”这个词，发现它首次出现在E段，并描述为一种寄生霉菌，暗示其可能导致感染和危险。 


解题思路：在E段中，句子提到Currie发现蚂蚁花园远非无感染，并分离出包括Escovopsis在内的外来生物，Escovopsis被特别指出是一种寄生霉菌。这间接暗示了Escovopsis的危险性，因为寄生霉菌通常会导致有害后果，如感染和破坏。虽然F段更详细描述了具体后果，但E段首次引入Escovopsis并关联其作为病原体的角色，因此包含了题目所问的信息。
21

21 the disadvantage of growing single fungus
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：The pure strain of fungus grown by the leaf-cutters, it seemed to Mr Currie, resembled the monocultures of various human crops, that are very productive for a while and then succumb to some disastrous pathogen, such as the Irish potato blight. Monocultures, which lack the genetic diversity to respond to changing environmental threats, are sitting ducks for parasites.


审题：题目询问种植单一真菌的缺点是什么，即单一真菌种植带来的不利之处。


定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"disadvantage"（缺点）和"growing single fungus"（种植单一真菌）进行定位。文章中，"single fungus"对应D段中的"pure strain"（纯菌株）或"monocultures"（单一栽培），"disadvantage"对应"succumb to some disastrous pathogen"（屈服于灾难性病原体）或"sitting ducks for parasites"（容易受到寄生虫攻击）。


解题思路：在D段中，作者描述leaf-cutting ants种植的纯菌株真菌类似于人类农业中的单一栽培，这些单一栽培虽然一开始高产，但由于缺乏遗传多样性，无法应对环境威胁，容易受到寄生虫和病原体的攻击，例如爱尔兰马铃薯枯萎病。这直接点明了种植单一真菌的缺点，即易受病原体侵害，导致灾难性后果。因此，D段包含了题目所问的信息。
22

22 comparison of features of two different nests
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：The leaf-cutters use fresh vegetation; the other groups, known as the lower attines because their nests are smaller and their techniques more primitive, feed their gardens with detritus like dead leaves, insects and faeces.


审题：题目要求找出文章中哪个段落包含了对两种不同巢穴特征的比较信息。


定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"comparison"（比较）、"features"（特征）和"two different nests"（两种不同巢穴）进行定位。在文章中扫描描述蚂蚁巢穴差异的部分，特别是提到leaf-cutters和lower attines的段落。


解题思路：在C段中，作者明确比较了leaf-cutting ants和lower attines ants的巢穴特征。句子中指出lower attines的巢穴更小（nests are smaller）、技术更原始（techniques more primitive），并且对比了它们喂养花园的方式（leaf-cutters使用新鲜植被，lower attines使用碎屑如死叶、昆虫和粪便）。这直接对应了题目中要求的对两种巢穴特征的比较，因此确定答案为C段。
23

23 two achievements made by ants earlier than human
✅ 正确答案：H

原文答案句：If so, some Alexander Fleming of an ant discovered antibiotics millions of years before people did. Even now, the ants are accomplishing two feats beyond the powers of human technology. The leaf-cutters are growing a monocultural crop year after year without disaster, and they are using an antibiotic apparently so wisely and prudently that, unlike people, they are not provoking antibiotic resistance in the target pathogen.


审题：题目询问蚂蚁比人类更早实现的两个成就所在段落。  


定位关键信息：利用题目关键词“achievements”（成就）和“earlier than human”（比人类早）扫读段落，H段提到蚂蚁早于人类发现抗生素。 

 

解题思路：H段描述了蚂蚁的成就，如早在人类之前的数百万年前，某些蚂蚁就已发现抗生素。至今，它们仍在完成两项人类尚未做到的成就：切叶蚁能够长期稳定地种植单一作物而不遭毁灭性打击；并且，它们极为谨慎地使用抗生素，从而避免了人类频繁面对的耐药性问题。
24

24 advantage of growing a new breed of fungus
✅ 正确答案：F

原文答案句：There is now a possible reason to explain why the lower attine species keep changing the variety of fungus in their mushroom gardens, and occasionally domesticating new ones – to stay one step ahead of the relentless Escovopsis.


对应段落：F段  


审题：题目问的是“培育新品种真菌的优势”，即寻找关于培育新真菌带来的好处或积极影响的信息。  


定位关键信息：利用题目中的"new breed of fungus"（新品种真菌）定位，在文章中寻找与培育新真菌相关的正面描述或优势。  


解题思路：根据上文可知Escovopsis霉菌对真菌农场的破坏性影响，并提到其他蚂蚁不会靠近被感染的区域，导致相关蚂蚁因饥饿而死亡。F段最后提到了lower attine species不断更换“蘑菇园”中的真菌品种，并偶尔驯化新的真菌——这是为了始终领先于穷追不舍的Escovopsis一步。

Questions 25-26

Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

25

How does the author think of Currie’s opinion?

正确答案： A. His viewpoint was verified later.

原文答案句：But after three years of sifting through attine ant gardens, Mr Currie discovered they are far from free of infection.  


审题：题目询问作者对Currie观点的看法，具体是Currie认为蚂蚁真菌系统中存在寄生虫的观点。  


定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词“Currie”和“opinion”定位到材料D段和E段。D段提到Currie的观点（认为有寄生虫）最初不被支持，E段描述了他的研究发现验证了这一观点。  


解题思路：在D段，Currie felt there had to be a parasite in the ant-fungus system，但a century of ant research offered no support for the idea，表明他的观点最初缺乏证据。在E段，Currie经过三年研究discovered they are far from free of infection，并分离出Escovopsis等病原体，这直接验证了他的观点。作者通过叙述这一从怀疑到证实的过程，表明Currie的观点后来被实验证据支持，因此对应选项A（His viewpoint was verified later）。

26

What did scientists find on the skin of ants under a microscope?

正确答案： C. a substance which is useful to humans

原文答案句：Mr Currie discovered a specialized patch on the ants’ cuticle that harbors a particular kind of bacterium, one well known to the pharmaceutical industry, because it is the source of half the antibiotics used in medicine.  


审题：题目询问科学家在蚂蚁皮肤下显微镜观察时发现了什么。  


定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"skin"（对应原文中的"cuticle"，指昆虫体表）和"under a microscope"（原文中直接出现"put these things under a microscope"）定位到H段。  


解题思路：原文中，Currie将蚂蚁cuticle上的whitish growth置于显微镜下观察，发现它不是人们之前认为的cuticular wax，而是一种活的细菌。这种细菌是Streptomyces，在制药业中众所周知，因为它是医学中使用的半数抗生素的来源，因此对人类非常有用。这直接支持选项C"a substance which is useful to humans"，而其他选项不准确：A提到"white cloud mold"，但原文中是细菌而非霉菌；B提到"wax"，但原文否定它是蜡；D提到"suppresses the growth of fungus"，虽然细菌确实抑制Escovopsis霉菌，但主要焦点是它对人类的用途。
